name: Johor (Malaysia)

colors.symbolism: the Sultan, the head of state

description: A blue field with a red rectangle in the upper hoist canton, charged in the centre of the canton with a yellow crescent and a five-pointed yellow star.
history: The state flag of Johor in southern Peninsular Malaysia, in use since the late 19th century during the rule of the Johor Sultanate. The dark-blue field represents the people, the red canton the army, and the yellow crescent and star the Sultan and his Islamic faith.

features.symbolism: a red canton charged with a yellow crescent and star — Islam and the Sultan
